body:
# Issue 6.1: Web Camera & Capture UI Component

## Objective
Build a responsive, modern web camera viewfinder and photo capture component adhering to the application's glassmorphism dark design system. The component handles media stream initialization, fallback photo uploads, client-side geolocation gathering, sending requests to the core capture coordinator API (`/api/capture`), and rendering interactive response cards for discoveries/encounters.

## Design & UI Specifications
- **Theme Tokens**: Component styling must consume CSS design tokens defined in Issue #24 ([Issue 2.3: Design System & Dark Mode / Glassmorphism UI Theme Tokens](https://github.com/jamescraig2/gregduck/issues/24)).
- **Layout & Visuals**:
  - Full-width dark media container (`--color-bg-base`) with glassmorphism control overlays (`.glass-panel`, `--glass-backdrop-blur`).
  - Sleek floating shutter button with hover/active state animations (`--color-primary`, `--color-primary-glow`).
  - Live preview video stream container with rounded glass corners (`--radius-lg`) and crisp border (`--glass-border`).

## Component Architecture & Capture Workflow

### 1. Camera Feed & Fallback Management
- Primary method: Initialize video stream via `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: { facingMode: { exact: 'environment' } } })` (falling back to `{ video: true }`).
- Fallback method: If camera access is denied, unsupported, or insecure HTTP context, render standard `<input type="file" accept="image/*" capture="environment">` inside a custom styled glassmorphism button.
- Canvas capture: Render video frame onto an offscreen `<canvas>` context to generate a compressed Blob/Base64 image payload.

### 2. Geolocation Acquisition & Request Payload
- Trigger `navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition` upon shutter click (or utilize pre-fetched position cache with fallback prompt).
- Construct payload: `FormData` or JSON containing `image` Blob/Base64, `latitude`, `longitude`, and `accuracy`.
- Execute HTTP `POST` request to `/api/capture` endpoint (defined in Issue #16).

### 3. Loading Overlay & Async State Machine
- **Idle / Active Viewfinder**: Camera live feed visible, shutter button enabled.
- **Location / Capture Pending**: Shutter disabled, spinner with subtle glow (`--color-primary-glow`) and text status "Acquiring location & capturing frame...".
- **Gemini Evaluation Pending**: Translucent dark glass overlay (`--color-bg-surface` + `--glass-backdrop-blur`) with animated pulse shimmer ("Analyzing animal species with Gemini Vision...").
- **Error State**: Non-blocking toast notification or alert card detailing failure (e.g., GPS unavailable, Vision API failure, bad image) with retry trigger.

### 4. Result UI Cards
- **New Discovery Modal (`resultType === 'new_discovery'`)**:
  - Congratulatory header with emerald highlight (`--color-accent-emerald`).
  - Displays species name, visual confidence score, rarity badge, and optional fun facts.
  - Action buttons: "View in Journal" and "Dismiss".
- **Existing Animal Encounter Modal (`resultType === 'encounter'`)**:
  - "Say Hello Again!" greeting with amber highlight (`--color-accent-amber`).
  - Displays animal name/ID, encounter count increment notification, and relative distance update.
  - Action buttons: "Log Encounter" and "Dismiss".

## Dependencies & Blockers
- **Issue #16**: [Issue 5.3: Core Capture Coordinator Endpoint (/api/capture)](https://github.com/jamescraig2/gregduck/issues/16) - Required for server-side image processing, species evaluation, and database insertion.
- **Issue #24**: [Issue 2.3: Design System & Dark Mode / Glassmorphism UI Theme Tokens](https://github.com/jamescraig2/gregduck/issues/24) - Required for CSS custom properties, glassmorphism styles, and color palette tokens.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Viewfinder successfully requests and displays device camera feed with environment/rear camera preference.
- [ ] Graceful fallback to `<input type="file" capture="environment">` if `getUserMedia` is unsupported or rejected.
- [ ] Retrieves precise GPS coordinates (`latitude`, `longitude`) upon capturing image.
- [ ] Sends image payload and GPS coordinates to `POST /api/capture`.
- [ ] Displays translucent glass loading overlay with status text during API submission.
- [ ] Renders distinct UI modals/cards for **New Discovery** vs **Existing Encounter** based on API response payload.
- [ ] Fully styled using design tokens from Issue #24.

body:
# Issue 6.2: Google Maps Interface with Marker Clustering

## Objective
Implement an interactive Google Maps interface utilizing `@vis.gl/react-google-maps` to display user position, active H3 grid boundaries, and clustered animal markers fetched dynamically based on map viewport changes.

## Technical Specifications & Implementation Details

### 1. Map Component Setup (`@vis.gl/react-google-maps`)
- Use `<APIProvider>` with Google Maps API Key loaded from environment variables (`NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY`).
- Render `<Map>` component with standard dark/custom Map ID styling and user position marker using browser geolocation API.
- Support default center and zoom levels with smooth panning controls.

### 2. Viewport Boundary Change Detection & Automated Refetching
- Attach event listeners `onCameraChanged` / `onBoundsChanged` to detect user panning, zooming, or map movement.
- Extract map viewport bounding box coordinates (`sw_lat`, `sw_lng`, `ne_lat`, `ne_lng`) from map bounds instance (`map.getBounds()`).
- Debounce camera change events (e.g., 300ms) to prevent thrashing API calls.
- Fetch visible animal markers asynchronously from `GET /api/markers?sw_lat=...&sw_lng=...&ne_lat=...&ne_lng=...`.

### 3. Marker Clustering & Interactivity
- Integrate `@googlemaps/markerclusterer` or `@vis.gl/react-google-maps` clustering library for grouping high-density markers.
- Render custom marker pins with species-specific icon badges or animal avatars.
- Attach an `onClick` event handler to markers and cluster pins:
  - Clicking an individual animal marker opens a bottom drawer / teaser modal displaying animal name, species, thumbnail, distance, and last encounter timestamp.
  - Teaser card includes a link/button to navigate to the full animal detail profile (`/animals/[id]`).

## Tasks
- [ ] Install `@vis.gl/react-google-maps` and `@googlemaps/markerclusterer`.
- [ ] Build base Google Map dashboard component with geolocation tracking.
- [ ] Implement debounced viewport change handlers (`onCameraChanged` / `onBoundsChanged`).
- [ ] Connect map component to `GET /api/markers` for real-time marker refetching on viewport updates.
- [ ] Implement Marker Clustering for dense areas.
- [ ] Create Animal Teaser Card/Modal component triggered on marker click.
- [ ] Add unit and UI component tests for map state and modal interactions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Map renders properly with customized Google Maps styling and API provider initialization.
- [ ] Viewport shifts trigger debounced fetch requests to `GET /api/markers` with updated bounding box query parameters.
- [ ] Animal markers render on map and cluster appropriately when zoomed out.
- [ ] Clicking a marker displays the Animal Teaser Modal with correct animal details and navigation links.

## Dependencies & Blockers
- **Issue #6**: Database Schema & Connection Pool Setup (Neon + Drizzle) - Required for animal persistence.
- **Issue #12**: Spatial Indexing Service (h3-js) - Required for spatial grid index mapping.
- **Issue #23**: Animal Markers Viewport API Endpoint (`GET /api/markers`) - Backend precursor API to supply viewport-bounded marker data.
- **Design System / Component Library**: UI design tokens and base modal/drawer components for animal teaser card.

body:
# Issue 7.1: Discovery Journal (List View)

## Objective
Present users with a responsive, styled catalog of all unique animals they have discovered or encountered, featuring filtering, search, and tabbed navigation.

## UI Specifications
- **Tabbed Navigation**:
  - "My Discoveries": Displays unique species discovered for the first time by the authenticated user (`tab=discoveries`).
  - "All Encountered Animals": Chronological grid of all animal encounters logged by the user (`tab=all`).
- **Grid & Card Layout**:
  - Responsive multi-column layout (1 column mobile, 2-3 columns tablet, 4 columns desktop).
  - Animal Card components displaying:
    - Animal Name (heading)
    - Canonical Species Badge (styled status tag)
    - Discovery/Encounter Date (formatted relative/localized timestamp)
    - Photo preview thumbnail (with lazy loading & placeholder fallback)
    - "First Discovery" badge indicator (when applicable)
- **Search & Filter Controls**:
  - Species search input (real-time query parameter filtering).
  - Date range filter selector (Filter encounters by date range).
  - Sort selector (Newest First, Oldest First, Alphabetical).

## Data Fetching & Integration
- Fetch data client-side or server-side from `GET /api/journal` (Issue #21).
- Support pagination / infinite scroll using response pagination metadata (`page`, `limit`, `total`, `totalPages`).
- Handle loading states (skeleton grid cards), empty states ("No discoveries logged yet"), and error states with retry actions.

## Dependencies & Blockers
- **Issue #6**: Database Schema & Connection Pool Setup (Neon + Drizzle)
- **Issue #7**: Clerk Authentication & User Cache Synchronization
- **Issue #21**: User Discoveries & Encounters API Endpoint (`GET /api/journal`)
- **Design System Specifications**: Finalized UI component specifications and theme token definitions for premium styling.

## Tasks
- [ ] Implement tabbed layout container for "My Discoveries" vs "All Encountered Animals".
- [ ] Build search bar, species filter, and date picker controls.
- [ ] Create reusable `AnimalCard` component adhering to Design System UI tokens.
- [ ] Integrate data fetching layer connecting to `GET /api/journal`.
- [ ] Implement loading skeleton, empty state UI, and error handling states.
- [ ] Implement pagination / infinite scrolling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Switching tabs triggers accurate API request (`tab=discoveries` vs `tab=all`) and updates list UI.
- [ ] Filter and search controls filter the rendered animal grid dynamically.
- [ ] Cards display animal name, species badge, discovery date, photo preview, and discovery tags accurately.
- [ ] Page handles loading skeleton and empty states cleanly.

body:
# Issue 7.2: Animal Profile & Sighting Timeline

## Objective
Provide a rich, interactive full-screen drawer/modal detailing an individual animal's life story, AI backstory, initial discoverer badge, and subsequent visitor sighting timeline.

## UI & UX Specifications

### View Shell & Layout
- **Container**: Full-screen slide-over drawer on desktop, bottom-sheet / modal on mobile with smooth backdrop blur transitions.
- **Close & Navigation**: Accessible close button (`X`) and ESC key binding to dismiss the modal, returning focus to the map or journal view.

### Header Section
- **Hero Image**: Large high-resolution photo of the animal (first encounter photo), with fallback blur-up loading state.
- **Title & Species Badge**: Animal name (e.g., "Barnaby the Blue Heron") rendered with prominent typography, alongside a styled canonical species tag.
- **Discoverer Badge**: Highlighted badge showing the initial discoverer's profile avatar, display name, and initial discovery timestamp.
- **AI Backstory**: Styled narrative text card displaying the generated AI backstory with subtle background highlight and quotation formatting.

### Sighting Timeline Section
- **Timeline Structure**: Chronologically ordered vertical timeline of all visitor encounters.
- **Timeline Item Elements**:
  - Visitor avatar image & cached Clerk username.
  - Encounter date & time formatted in relative/localized timestamp (e.g. "3 days ago • July 20, 2026 2:15 PM").
  - Uploaded photo card from that specific encounter with lightboxed image view on click.
  - Optional encounter notes/comments submitted by the visitor.

## Data Fetching & Integration
- **Client-Side Data Fetching**: Fetch animal details and timeline by making a `GET /api/animals/[id]` request (Issue #22) when opening the modal for a specific `animalId`.
- **Loading & Error States**:
  - Show skeleton loading UI while fetching modal details.
  - Render a friendly error card with retry button if fetching fails or if `animalId` is invalid.

## Dependencies & Blockers
- **Issue #6**: Database Schema & Connection Pool Setup (Neon + Drizzle)
- **Issue #7**: Clerk Authentication & User Cache Synchronization
- **Issue #18**: Google Maps Interface with Marker Clustering
- **Issue #19**: Discovery Journal (List View)
- **Issue #22**: Animal Detail & Sighting Timeline API Endpoint (`GET /api/animals/[id]`)
- **Design System Specifications**: Finalized UI component specifications and theme token definitions for premium modal & timeline styling.

## Tasks
- [ ] Build responsive full-screen slide-over drawer / bottom-sheet modal component.
- [ ] Implement header section displaying hero image, animal name, species badge, discoverer profile, and AI backstory.
- [ ] Implement vertical timeline component displaying visitor encounters with avatars, timestamps, and photos.
- [ ] Integrate client-side data fetching calling `GET /api/animals/[id]`.
- [ ] Implement skeleton loading state and error handling UI.
- [ ] Connect trigger events from Google Maps markers (#18) and Discovery Journal list items (#19).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Modal opens when an animal marker or journal card is clicked, fetching data from `GET /api/animals/[id]`.
- [ ] Header displays hero image, species badge, AI backstory, and initial discoverer badge accurately.
- [ ] Sighting timeline renders historical encounters in chronological order with visitor avatars, formatted timestamps, and encounter photos.
- [ ] UI handles loading skeletons and fetch failure gracefully.

body:
# Issue 7.3: User Discoveries & Encounters API Endpoint (GET /api/journal)

## Objective
Implement the backend REST API endpoint (`GET /api/journal`) to fetch authenticated user discoveries and encounter records from the database.

## Technical Specifications
- **Authentication**: Perform user authentication using Clerk SDK (`auth()`) to retrieve `userId`. Return `401 Unauthorized` if unauthenticated.
- **Database Access & ORM**:
  - Query using Drizzle ORM joining `encounters` and `animals` tables filtered by `encounters.userId`.
  - Distinguish first-time discoveries (`isFirstDiscovery` flag or earliest encounter per animal) from overall encounter history.
- **Query Parameters**:
  - `tab`: `discoveries` (returns first-time unique animal discoveries) vs `all` (returns all logged encounters, default: `discoveries`).
  - `species`: Filter by species name / category string.
  - `startDate` / `endDate`: Filter encounters by ISO date ranges.
  - `page` / `limit`: Pagination parameters (default `page=1`, `limit=20`).
  - `search`: Case-insensitive text search on animal name or species.
- **Response Format**:
  - JSON payload containing:
    ```json
    {
      "data": [
        {
          "encounterId": "string",
          "animalId": "string",
          "name": "string",
          "species": "string",
          "discoveredAt": "string (ISO date)",
          "photoUrl": "string | null",
          "isFirstDiscovery": true
        }
      ],
      "pagination": {
        "page": 1,
        "limit": 20,
        "total": 42,
        "totalPages": 3
      }
    }
    ```

## Tasks
- [ ] Create API route handler at `app/api/journal/route.ts`.
- [ ] Integrate Clerk `auth()` middleware verification.
- [ ] Build Drizzle ORM relational query joining `encounters` and `animals` tables.
- [ ] Support filtering (`species`, `search`, `startDate`, `endDate`, `tab`) and pagination.
- [ ] Unit & Integration testing for authenticated/unauthenticated API calls.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unauthenticated requests receive HTTP `401 Unauthorized`.
- [ ] `GET /api/journal?tab=discoveries` accurately returns unique first-time discovered species per user.
- [ ] `GET /api/journal?tab=all` returns full chronological encounter list.
- [ ] Filtering and pagination parameters function accurately with clean metadata response.

## Dependencies
- Issue #6: Database Schema Definitions (`encounters` and `animals` tables)
- Issue #7: Clerk User Cache & Authentication Integration

labels.description: Issue has been planned and is ready for implementation

body:
# Issue 7.4: Animal Detail & Sighting Timeline API Endpoint (GET /api/animals/[id])

## Objective
Implement a RESTful API route handler (`GET /api/animals/[id]`) to retrieve detailed metadata for a specific animal along with a chronologically ordered list of all visitor sightings/encounters.

## API Specification

### Request
- **Method:** `GET`
- **Path:** `/api/animals/[id]`
- **Headers:** `Authorization: Bearer <clerk_token>` (if authenticated)

### Response Payload (`200 OK`)
```json
{
  "id": "anim_12345",
  "name": "Barnaby the Blue Heron",
  "species": "Great Blue Heron",
  "backstory": "First spotted near the edge of the lake...",
  "discoveredAt": "2026-07-01T10:00:00Z",
  "discoverer": {
    "userId": "user_abc",
    "name": "Jane Doe",
    "avatarUrl": "https://img.clerk.com/..."
  },
  "encounters": [
    {
      "id": "enc_001",
      "createdAt": "2026-07-01T10:00:00Z",
      "photoUrl": "https://storage.googleapis.com/...",
      "notes": "Initial discovery near the pier.",
      "user": {
        "userId": "user_abc",
        "name": "Jane Doe",
        "avatarUrl": "https://img.clerk.com/..."
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "enc_002",
      "createdAt": "2026-07-05T14:30:00Z",
      "photoUrl": "https://storage.googleapis.com/...",
      "notes": "Spotted flying low near the reeds.",
      "user": {
        "userId": "user_xyz",
        "name": "John Smith",
        "avatarUrl": "https://img.clerk.com/..."
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Error Responses
- `404 Not Found`: `{ "error": "Animal not found" }`
- `500 Internal Server Error`: `{ "error": "Failed to fetch animal details" }`

## Requirements
1. Query database for animal entity metadata (name, species, AI backstory, initial discoverer user ID).
2. Query associated `encounters` table for all encounters linked to `animal_id`, ordered by `createdAt` timestamp.
3. Resolve user profile information (display name, avatar URL) for discoverer and encounterers using Clerk User Cache (#7).
4. Return structured JSON payload.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Route handler correctly handles valid animal ID and returns complete metadata and encounter timeline.
- [ ] Returns 404 for invalid/non-existent animal ID.
- [ ] Includes enriched user profiles (name, avatar) for all encounter entries.

labels.name: planned
labels.description: Issue has been planned and is ready for implementation

body:
# Issue 6.3: Animal Markers Viewport API Endpoint (GET /api/markers)

## Objective
Implement a RESTful GET API endpoint (`/api/markers`) that serves animal location markers bounded by the client map's viewport coordinates or H3 spatial index cell bounds.

## Requirements & Specifications

### Query Parameters
The endpoint must support filtering by geographic bounds:
- **Bounding Box Parameters**:
  - `sw_lat`: South-West Latitude (float, e.g. `-90.0` to `90.0`)
  - `sw_lng`: South-West Longitude (float, e.g. `-180.0` to `180.0`)
  - `ne_lat`: North-East Latitude (float, e.g. `-90.0` to `90.0`)
  - `ne_lng`: North-East Longitude (float, e.g. `-180.0` to `180.0`)
- **H3 Cell Parameters (Optional alternative)**:
  - `h3_indexes`: Comma-separated list of H3 cell index strings representing the visible area.

### Database Query
- Query the `animals` table using Drizzle ORM.
- Filter records where animal latitude (`lat`) and longitude (`lng`) fall within `[sw_lat, ne_lat]` and `[sw_lng, ne_lng]` (handling antimeridian wrapping if applicable).
- Include metadata such as animal ID, name, species, last encounter timestamp, preview image URL, and location coordinates.

### Response Format
- HTTP Status `200 OK`
- JSON structure:
```json
{
  "markers": [
    {
      "id": "string",
      "name": "string",
      "species": "string",
      "lat": 0.0,
      "lng": 0.0,
      "h3Index": "string",
      "lastEncounteredAt": "ISO-8601 timestamp",
      "imageUrl": "string"
    }
  ],
  "total": 0
}
```

### Error Handling
- Return `400 Bad Request` if coordinates are invalid, out of bounds, or missing required parameters.
- Return `500 Internal Server Error` on database connection or execution failures.

## Tasks
- [ ] Define Zod schema for query parameter validation (`sw_lat`, `sw_lng`, `ne_lat`, `ne_lng`, `h3_indexes`).
- [ ] Implement Drizzle ORM query logic to select animals within bounding box / H3 indexes.
- [ ] Build Next.js API route handler for `GET /api/markers`.
- [ ] Add unit tests and integration tests validating coordinate filtering and response formatting.

## Dependencies & Blockers
- **Issue 6**: DB Schema (Drizzle ORM `animals` table setup).
- **Issue 12**: H3 Spatial Indexing (Spatial indexing utilities and H3 coordinate mappings).

labels.name: planned
labels.description: Issue has been planned and is ready for implementation

labels.description: Backend endpoints and API routing

body:
# Issue 2.3: Design System & Dark Mode / Glassmorphism UI Theme Tokens

## Objective
Establish a centralized, cohesive CSS variable design system and theme token architecture specifying the dark mode aesthetic, glassmorphism components, color palette, typography, elevation, and loading state visuals across the application.

## Specifications & CSS Tokens

### 1. Core Color Palette
- `--color-bg-base`: `#0B0F17` (Deep space dark background)
- `--color-bg-surface`: `rgba(18, 24, 38, 0.7)` (Translucent glass panel surface)
- `--color-bg-card`: `rgba(25, 34, 52, 0.65)` (Elevated glass card surface)
- `--color-primary`: `#3B82F6` (Electric Blue)
- `--color-primary-glow`: `rgba(59, 130, 246, 0.35)`
- `--color-accent-emerald`: `#10B981` (Vibrant Emerald / Discovery highlight)
- `--color-accent-amber`: `#F59E0B` (Encounter highlight)
- `--color-text-main`: `#F3F4F6` (High contrast primary text)
- `--color-text-muted`: `#9CA3AF` (Secondary text)
- `--color-text-subtle`: `#6B7280` (Muted/disabled text)

### 2. Glassmorphism & Effects
- `--glass-backdrop-blur`: `blur(16px) saturate(180%)`
- `--glass-border`: `1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12)`
- `--glass-border-highlight`: `1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25)`
- `--glass-box-shadow`: `0 8px 32px 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.37)`
- `--glass-inset-glow`: `inset 0 1px 1px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1)`

### 3. Typography & Hierarchy
- `--font-sans`: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif
- `--font-mono`: 'JetBrains Mono', Fira Code, monospace
- Font scales: Heading 1 (`2rem`/`1.2`), Heading 2 (`1.5rem`/`1.3`), Body (`1rem`/`1.5`), Caption (`0.875rem`/`1.4`)

### 4. Elevation, Radii & Card Borders
- `--radius-sm`: `6px`
- `--radius-md`: `12px`
- `--radius-lg`: `20px`
- `--radius-full`: `9999px`
- Card elevation layers with subtle borders (`--glass-border`) and rounded glass container corners.

### 5. Loading States & Micro-Animations
- Shimmer keyframes and translucent pulse overlays (`--color-loading-pulse: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05)`).
- Loading spinner animation with primary glow (`--color-primary-glow`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Define global CSS variables in `app/globals.css` (or central theme system).
- [ ] Provide utility classes or token imports for glassmorphism panels (`.glass-panel`, `.glass-card`).
- [ ] Verify dark mode visual hierarchy, contrast ratios, and loading keyframe utility classes.
